title: gymnogramma brunoniana
intro: Gymnogramma brunoniana, also known as the Devil's backbone or Gymnogramma, is a type of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a succulent plant that grows in the tropical regions of Asia and is often used in traditional medicine. The plant is reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to provide various health benefits such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and aiding in wound healing. Herbal preparations of Gymnogramma brunoniana include infusions and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
